title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye floaters
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye floaters are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate the appearance of small spots or threads floating in one's vision. These tinctures work by improving blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation, and enhancing eye health. The best herbal tinctures for eye floaters include Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, Centella asiatica, Zingiber officinale, Ruscus aculeatus, Curcuma longa, Vitis vinifera, Avena sativa, Boswellia serrata, and Viscum album. If left untreated, eye floaters can significantly impact daily life, causing vision disturbances, eye strain, and decreased quality of life, even affecting one's ability to drive or perform daily tasks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with eye floaters because it contains a unique blend of bioactive compounds that have been shown to effectively alleviate the symptoms associated with vitreous detachment, a common cause of eye floaters. The tincture's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the visibility of floaters. Additionally, its ability to improve blood flow and circulation may also help to reduce the appearance of floaters by promoting healthy retinal function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals in the body, including those that can damage the eyes and contribute to eye floaters.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory compounds, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, help improve blood circulation to the eyes, which may reduce the occurrence of eye floaters by promoting healthy retinal tissue and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation in the body, including in the eyes, which can contribute to eye floaters by irritating the retina and vitreous gel.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative is believed to help with eye floaters by improving blood flow to the retina and reducing inflammation, which can help to alleviate the appearance of floaters.

Ginkgo flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant properties that can help to protect the retina from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the appearance of eye floaters.

Ginkgolide A (a sesquiterpene lactone): This compound has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the eye and alleviate the appearance of eye floaters, possibly by improving the clarity of the vitreous gel that contains the floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce floaters.

Seeds: The seeds are also used for their potential to improve vision and reduce floaters due to their high content of ginkgolides and bilobalide, which may help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
Buds: Some herbal remedies use ginkgo buds as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that may help reduce eye floaters and improve vision by improving blood flow to the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall when they are fully ripe and have turned yellow.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 part of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with eye floaters because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The high concentration of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids in blueberries work together to reduce the formation of free radicals that can damage the vitreous humor and cause floaters. Additionally, blueberry tincture's anti-inflammatory effects help to alleviate any underlying inflammation or irritation that may be contributing to the appearance of eye floaters. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to address this common issue without the need for surgery or medication.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and worsening of eye floaters.

Fisetin: This flavonoid compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce the accumulation of amyloid beta-protein, a protein that is associated with age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and may contribute to the development of eye floaters.

Quercetin: As an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, quercetin may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can lead to improved blood vessel health and reduced symptoms of eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to the visibility of eye floaters.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help reduce inflammation in the eyes and improve overall eye health.
Delphinidin: A type of anthocyanin with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, delphinidin may help reduce the appearance of eye floaters by protecting the delicate tissues of the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Vaccinium myrtillus are used to make tinctures for eye floaters because they contain anthocyanins, which may help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the eyes.

Fruits: The fruits of Vaccinium myrtillus are used to make tinctures for eye floaters because they are rich in anthocyanins and other antioxidants that may help improve vision and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.

Leaves: The leaves of Vaccinium myrtillus are also used to make tinctures for eye floaters because they contain flavonoids, which may help protect the retina and reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of vaccinium myrtillus berries and 1 part of the leaves in the summer months for best results.
Dry the harvested vaccinium myrtillus mixture in a warm place for 1-2 weeks or until completely dry.
Combine 1 part of the dried vaccinium myrtillus mixture with 2 parts of a 40% glycerin solution in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the glycerin solution for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with eye floaters because of its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the eyes. The herbal extract is believed to soothe and calm the delicate tissues of the retina, which can become irritated by the presence of floaters. Additionally, eyebright's antioxidant properties may help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the formation of new floaters. By promoting healthy blood flow and tissue integrity, eyebright tinctures may help to alleviate eye discomfort and improve overall vision clarity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters such as eye strain and irritation.
Antioxidant properties: Eyebright's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.

Vasodilatory properties: Eyebright's vasodilatory compounds help improve blood flow to the eyes, which can increase oxygenation and nutrition to the retina, potentially reducing the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the visibility of eye floaters.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin and Kaempferol): These plant-based antioxidants help protect the eye's delicate tissues from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms of eye floaters.

Iridoid Glycosides (including Euphrasin and Ajugoside): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the appearance and frequency of eye floaters by soothing and protecting the eye's delicate tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Euphrasia officinalis are commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help address eye health issues, including eye floaters.
Flowers: The flowers are used as they contain a significant amount of flavonoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for eye health.
Stems: The stems of Euphrasia officinalis are also used, as they contain a mix of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 to 40 grams of fresh euphrasia officinalis flowers or 15 to 20 grams of dried flowers.
Chop the euphrasia officinalis flowers finely to release their active compounds and prepare them for extraction.
Combine the chopped euphrasia officinalis flowers with 70 to 80 percent vodka and let the mixture steep for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the plant material.
Transfer the herbal euphrasia officinalis tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with eye floaters because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is often associated with the formation of eye floaters. By reducing inflammation, pot marigold tinctures may help alleviate symptoms such as blurry vision, eye fatigue, and increased sensitivity to light, ultimately improving overall visual clarity and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to the formation of eye floaters.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the vitreous humor, a gel-like substance that fills the center of the eye and is where eye floaters appear.

Vasodilatory properties: Pot marigold's vasodilatory effects can help improve blood flow to the eyes, which may help reduce the appearance of eye floaters by increasing the clearance of debris from the vitreous humor.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigments help to improve the health and clarity of the retina, which can help alleviate the appearance of eye floaters.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity of symptoms associated with eye floaters, such as eye strain and discomfort.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the retina and optic nerve from damage, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes.
Leaves: They contain a high concentration of antioxidants and saponins, which aid in reducing oxidative stress and improving vision clarity.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to treat eye issues due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which may help to reduce eye inflammation and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh calendula flowers and 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the calendula flowers in the vodka for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and store the liquid in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks.
Use the tincture as needed by adding a few drops to water or other liquids for medicinal purposes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with eye floaters because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken as a supplement, these compounds can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which may contribute to the formation of floaters. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidants may also help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from oxidative damage, allowing for clearer vision and reducing the appearance of floaters.

remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica may help reduce eye floaters by improving blood flow to the eyes and reducing inflammation.

Phenolics (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): Phenolics, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, in Urtica dioica may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which could contribute to eye floaters.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the urtica dioica leaves and stems during the early morning when the plant is at its highest potency level.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its volatile oils and essence.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the mixture to steep.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with eye floaters because its soothing properties calm inflammation in the eyes, reducing the appearance of floating particles. The herb's mucilages also lubricate and protect the delicate tissues within the eyes, allowing for smoother blood flow and reduced pressure on the retina. Leaves: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress, a factor contributing to eye floaters.
Stems: Rich in polysaccharides and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried root material of the desired herb in the morning when it is dry.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped material with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass container with a tight lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compounds in goldenseal, such as berberine, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the eyes and improve circulation, which can help dissolve or remove the protein clumps that cause eye floaters. Additionally, the antioxidant effects of goldenseal may also help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from oxidative damage, further reducing the likelihood of floaters forming.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal's berberine compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes that may contribute to eye floaters.

Berbanthine: The berbanthine alkaloid found in goldenseal has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can improve blood flow and reduce pressure within the eyes, potentially reducing the occurrence of eye floaters caused by abnormal vessel growth or blockage.

Saponins: Goldenseal's saponin content is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and infection in the eyes, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with eye floaters such as vision distortion and sensitivity.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (Specifically Berberine): Berberine, an alkaloid present in goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the eye and alleviate symptoms of eye floaters.

Iridoids (Specifically Ursolic Acid): Ursolic acid, an iridoid found in goldenseal, has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floater symptoms.

Phenolic Acids (Specifically Gallic Acid): Gallic acid, a phenolic acid in goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floater symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, a bioactive compound believed to improve vision and reduce eye floaters.
Leaves: They are also utilized, as they contain similar levels of berberine as the roots, making them a viable alternative for eye floater tinctures.
Rhyzomes: They are sometimes used as a substitute for roots, as they share similar properties and contain berberine, which is thought to aid in reducing eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried roots of the plant in the fall season after the first frost.
Chop the harvested roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 part of chopped roots with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate eye floater symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried curcuma longa roots and stems to make herbal tinctures.
Combine the 2 cups of dried curcuma longa roots and stems with 4 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the solids.
Store the final tincture in the dark glass container and label it clearly for future use and shelf life of 5 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitis vinifera leaves in the morning when they are at their highest concentration of active compounds.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Combine 1000 grams of the cleaned vitis vinifera leaves with 5 liters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

This can help alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters, such as blurred vision and flashing lights, by reducing the inflammatory response that contributes to their formation. Antioxidant properties: Borage leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the formation of eye floaters.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh or dried borago officinalis flowers and leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned borago officinalis with 500 milliliters of 60 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow the mixture to steep for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with eye floaters because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken regularly, it reduces inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can cause or exacerbate eye floaters. Additionally, licorice root tincture may help to relax the tiny fibers in the vitreous gel inside the eye, reducing their tendency to cast shadows on the retina, making floaters more noticeable. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote overall eye health and alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby reducing the visibility of eye floaters.

Isoliquiritigenin: This compound has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the vitreous humor (the gel-like substance filling the center of the eye) that can contribute to the formation and appearance of eye floaters.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in amounts of 500 grams and transfer them to a clean glass jar.
Combine the glycyrrhiza glabra roots with high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a ratio of 1:3 parts plant-to-liquid.
Allow the mixture to steep for eight weeks at room temperature while occasionally shaking the jar to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid from the solids using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after pressing out as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried red clover flowers according to sustainable wildcrafting practices.
Combine one part red clover flowers with four parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar at room temperature for six weeks, shaking daily for 30 seconds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator for up to one year.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Combine one part taraxacum officinale plant material with two parts vodka at a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Pack the mixture into a clean glass jar leaving approximately one inch of headspace and secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks or more allowing the tincture to mature and infuse slowly.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transfer the liquid tincture to a clean glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when they are most potent for eye health.
Chop 50 grams of fresh leaves or 25 grams of dried leaves into small pieces using scissors and a clean surface.
Combine chopped herbs with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar and stir well to combine.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every two days to macerate the herbs.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tannins, flavonoids, and volatile oils in Kansas coneflower have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with eye floaters.

remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the spring or fall, ensuring freshness and quality of the herbs.

remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried valerian root valeriana officinalis and 500ml of high-proof vodka.
Combine the valerian root with 500ml of cold water in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place allowing for infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle.
Store the tincture in an airtight container at room temperature and use within one year.

remedy_recipe: Harvest ulmus rubra bark and leaves at dawn or dusk for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 7-10 days to preserve.
Combine one part dried ulmus rubra with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar and let it sit for 4 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids after 2 hours.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place protected from light for up to one year taking 20-30 drops twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh harpagophytum procumbens roots in the morning when they are at their peak potency and cleanliness.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with cool running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar or container filled with high-proof vodka at a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the final product in a dark container at room temperature.
